title: author-agent-spec YAML serializer folds multi-line prompts to a single line, destroying newlines
description: author-agent-spec at packages/pi-agent-dispatch/src/author-agent-spec-tool.ts line 72 (or line 88 per the investigation report's read) calls `yamlStringify(specObj)` with no options. The yaml package's default scalar style for multi-line strings is folded block scalar (`>-`), which collapses embedded `\n` characters to spaces. A prompt authored as `'line1\nline2'` becomes a single folded line in the written YAML. The author has no control over scalar style at the call site; no options are threaded through the tool surface to force literal block scalar (`|`) preservation of newlines.

evidence.reference: FB-002 'author-agent-spec YAML serializer uses folded block scalar, destroying newlines'; actual_behavior: prompt 'Read doc.\nPath is X.' becomes single folded line 'Read doc. Path is X.'; after folding the '/' triggers the FB-001 template-path heuristic

Investigation report FB-002 section confirms yamlStringify call site verbatim; verdict 'Code (tool parameter)' — pass `{lineWidth: -1, blockQuote: 'literal'}` options to yamlStringify to force literal block scalar preserving newlines
const yamlContent = yamlStringify(specObj); — no options passed, yaml package default folded-scalar style applies

impact: Multi-line prompts authored via author-agent-spec lose their newline structure at the serialization boundary. Multi-paragraph instructions, numbered workflows, and explicit blank-line separations are collapsed to single folded lines. Compounds with FGAP for FB-001: the author cannot use embedded newlines as an escape from the resolvePromptField '/' heuristic because the serializer strips the newlines before the YAML reaches disk. Authors have no diagnostic indication that their newline structure was lost.
proposed_resolution: Code at packages/pi-agent-dispatch/src/author-agent-spec-tool.ts line 72: pass options to yamlStringify to control scalar style — `yamlStringify(specObj, { lineWidth: -1, blockQuote: 'literal' })` per the investigation-report verdict. Exact option-name verification requires runtime check of the installed yaml package version; if option names differ, the fix shape stays the same — force literal-block-scalar serialization for multi-line strings, disable line wrapping.
